addressbook.title: Dr
addressbook.fulladdress: Ashwood Medical Group 448 Warrigal Road Ashburton VIC 3147

consult.history: New consult 31M Project manager at NAB Married, expecting first baby in November Previously well No meds NKDA Non-smoker Long-standing Gastrointestinal Sx since around 2009 Fatigue, lethargy - usually worse with Etoh Night sweats Broken sleep Abdo pain Hives in hands Rash on cheeks and lips Abdo bloating Previously constipated Saw gastroenterologist Colon 2012 - polyps Tried low FODMAPs with dietitian Tried probiotics Gas / colon 2014 normal incl Bx Bloating and constipation managed with diet No progressive weight loss Occ bright red blood PR No FHx CRC/polyps
consult.plan: Bloods US Phone for results Try probiotics, iberogast etc

consult.history: Appt on 3/12/2025 at: 10:40 AM - DELETED Actioned by Aileen Sneddon

downloadedresult.result: FULL BLOOD EXAMINATION (x10\S\9 /L) HB : 14.9 g/dL (13.0-18.0) * WHITE CELL COUNT: \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\3.7\R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ (4.0-11.0) PCV: 43.0 % (40.0-54.0) * Neutrophils: 41% \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\1.5\R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ (2.0-8.0) RCC: 4.74 x10\S\12 /L(4.50-6.50) Lymphocytes: 48% 1.8 (1.0-4.0) MCV: 91 fL (80-96) Monocytes : 8% 0.3 (0.0-1.0) MCH: 31.4 pg (27.0-32.0) Eosinophils: 2% 0.1 (0.0-0.5) MCHC 34.7 g/dL (32.0-36.0) Basophils : 1% 0.0 (0.0-0.2) RDW: 13.0 % (11.0-16.0) PLATELETS : 183 (150-450) \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\COMMENT:\R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ There is a mild neutropenia. Requested Tests : GS, TFT, FES*, CRP, MBI, FBE, ESU*, CLC*, BFO

THYROID FUNCTION TESTS (SERUM) Ref.Range Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) : 1.11 mIU/L (0.50-4.00) Normal TSH is consistent with normal thyroid function. Medical professionals: Please contact a pathologist on 03 9244 0380 if required. Requested Tests : GS, TFT, FES*, CRP, MBI, FBE, ESU*, CLC*, BFO

SERUM GLUCOSE Specimen Result Ref.Range Unknown 9:45 AM 5.0 mmol/L (4.0-7.8) Note (1): Glucose reference ranges: Fasting 4.0-6.0, Random 4.0-7.8. Consider further investigation including glucose tolerance test in people with fasting glucose 5.5-6.9 or random 7.8-11.0 mmol/L Requested Tests : GS, TFT, FES*, CRP, MBI, FBE, ESU*, CLC*, BFO

VITAMIN B12 AND FOLATE \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\Date: 21/09/15 \R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\Time: 09:45 \R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\Lab.No: 43842832 \R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\Ref.\R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\Units Range\R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ Serum Folate : 41.0 nmol/L (> 9.0) Requested Tests : GS, TFT, FES*, CRP, MBI, FBE, ESU*, CLC*, BFO
SERUM C-REACTIVE PROTEIN (CRP) Ref Range Date Lab. No. CRP (0-10) 21/09/15 43842832 < 1 mg/L Requested Tests : GS, TFT, FES*, CRP, MBI, FBE, ESU*, CLC*, BFO

SERUM/PLASMA BIOCHEMISTRY Ref.Range Sodium : 138 mmol/L (136-146) Potassium : 4.2 mmol/L (3.5-5.0) Chloride : 102 mmol/L (95-110) Bicarbonate : 31 mmol/L (22-31) Urea : 5.7 mmol/L (2.3-7.6) Est.GFR(mL/min) : > 90 per 1.73sqm(> 60) Creatinine : 74 umol/L (60-110) Total Bilirubin : 20 umol/L (< 20) Ala. Aminotransferase (ALT) : 18 U/L (< 55) Asp. Aminotransferase (AST) : 25 U/L (< 40) Alkaline Phosphatase (ALP) : 64 U/L (35-110) Gamma Glutamyl Trans. (GGT) : 19 U/L (< 40) Total Protein : 72 g/L (65-85) Albumin : 45 g/L (38-50) Globulin : 27 g/L (22-38) Calcium : 2.35 mmol/L (2.15-2.65) Cor. Calcium : 2.25 mmol/L (2.15-2.65) Phosphate : 1.1 mmol/L (0.8-1.4) Magnesium : 0.88 mmol/L (0.60-1.10) Requested Tests : GS, TFT, FES*, CRP, MBI, FBE, ESU*, CLC*, BFO

\R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\COELIAC DISEASE SEROLOGY (SERUM)\R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ Deamidated Gliadin IgG Ab (Bioplex): Not detected t-Transglutaminase IgA Ab (Bioplex): Not detected COMMENT: A new diagnosis of coeliac disease is unlikely with negative IgA transglutaminase and IgG deamidated gliadin peptide antibodies. This result may, however, be seen in patients with coeliac disease on a gluten-free diet, and may be useful for monitoring response to gluten exclusion. If clinical suspicion of a new diagnosis of coeliac disease is high, consider repeat serological assessment following 2-3 months on a gluten-containing diet. Clinical correlation is recommended. As of 18/11/2014, the method for initial screening has been changed. All positive results on screening will be checked and reported by current ELISA method to allow ongoing comparison with previous results. Requested Tests : GS, TFT, FES, CRP, MBI, FBE, ESU, CLC, BFO

IRON STUDIES \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\Date:21/09/15 \R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\Time:09:45 \R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\Lab.No:43842832 \R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\Ref.\R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\Units Range\R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ Ferritin: 104 ug/L (20-300) Iron: \R\SBLD\R\\R\FG04\R\35\R\EBLD\R\\R\FG99\R\ umol/L (10-30) Transferrin: 3.0 g/L (2.2-3.7) Transferrin Sat: 47 % (13-47) Elevated iron in association with normal ferritin is suggestive of iron therapy. Otherwise suggest repeat test to exclude possible iron excess. If interpretative assistance is required please contact a pathologist on (03) 9244 0380. Requested Tests : GS, TFT, FES, CRP, MBI, FBE, ESU, CLC*, BFO
Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate (Blood) ESR (Westergren): 2 mm/hr (< 15) Requested Tests : GS, TFT, FES*, CRP, MBI, FBE, ESU, CLC*, BFO

task.note: Hi Chamara, I accidentally put him in and deleted him straightaway. It was just an error. He hasnt been in this clinic since 2015. Message from :Dr Chamara Basnayake on 27/11/2025 at 07:47:39 ------------------- i saw this pt was deleted but ? no referal on genie
